meaning: adj.假设的,假定的,爱猜想的
examples: I wasn't asking about anybody in particular—it was a purely hypothetical question.
No doubt this hypothetical pattern did apply at times during the course of the recovery.
Whoever formulated the theory of the origin of the universe, it is just hypothetical and needs proving.
This hypothetical individual maintains a 75% stock and 25% bond allocation their entire working life.
Among its other arguments, Softbank says the hypothetical Dish-Sprint entity would be dangerously overleveraged.
